fault_hypothesis: The conflict detection condition on line 62 uses strict inequalities (`b <= s` and `a >= e`), which allows touching boundaries (e.g., b==s or a==e) to pass without conflict. The condition should use strict less-than (`b < s` and `a > e`) so that touching boundaries are also treated as conflicts.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline) or any(not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: When the new window touches an existing slot boundary (b==s or a==e), the conflict check must also return True (detect conflict); this fails when touching boundaries are incorrectly allowed.

spec.expr: all((b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) or (b__1 == s or a__1 == e) for s, e in timeline) == False or len(timeline) == 0

constraints.intent: After b is defined, verify that touching boundaries (b==s or a==e) are not silently treated as non-conflicting by the strict-inequality check.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s for s, e in timeline) and not any(a__1 == e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: Before b is used in the conflict check, assert that no touching boundary exists; if this is false, the conflict check on line 62 must detect it (which it currently fails to do).

spec.expr: all(b__1 < s or a__1 > e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After the non-conflict branch (else), assert that the new window is strictly non-touching with all existing slots; touching boundaries should not reach this branch.
